title: Bomarea declinata
taxonomy: Bomarea declinata is classified within the family Alstroemeriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the lily of the valley family. The genus Bomarea is a group of flowering plants found in South America, often referred to as the "glory of the forest." The species Bomarea declinata is native to Peru, where it thrives in the understory of tropical forests. It is also part of the order Asparagales, which includes many other types of flowering plants.
uses: Bomarea declinata is a plant species native to the Andean region, and like many other plants in this family, it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
